creator: cy@FreeBSD.org
text:
Have a patch that solves this:

bob# /usr/libexec/kadmind --debug
2022-11-26T16:16:51 root@EXAMPLE.ORG: CHPASS_WITH_KEY @
2022-11-26T16:16:51 CHPASS_WITH_KEY: Operation requires `change-password' privilege
Segmentation fault (core dumped)
bob# 

Unfortunately there's a memory error, circumvented by,
.
bob# MALLOC_CONF=zero:true /usr/libexec/kadmind --debug
2022-11-26T16:16:30 root@EXAMPLE.ORG: CHPASS_WITH_KEY @
2022-11-26T16:16:30 CHPASS_WITH_KEY: Operation requires `change-password' privilege
bob#

In other words fixing this bug has uncovered another error in which uninitialized memory is assumed to be zero. The error occurs in functions called by OPENSSL_cleanup(), an atexit() routine. I'm unsure at the moment if OpenSSL is the cause or simply a victim.

The error only occurs in debug mode, not when daemonized.
